Tool Usage — Run #1 (min tools among verifier-passed) (48 calls)
search_emails13
conversations_search_messages11

TodoWrite3

Prompt
U
Oracle Events
OE 1: Search emails for Heartland rate increase. Use search_emails(query: "Heartland") or search_emails(query: "rate increase") or search_emails(query: "Jake Loomis"). Agent finds email from Jake Loomis (jake.loomis@heartlandmovers.com), subject relating to "Formal Notice: Q3 2026 Rate Adjustment and Account Review" (or similar). Key details: 20% rate increase across all service tiers (linehaul, packing, storage, shuttle, accessorials) effective July 1, 2026. Heartland ties the increase to the unresolved Q1 billing dispute (HM-2026-Q1-0042), questioning MoveOps as a "dependable partner" and threatening to deprioritize scheduling in Midwest lanes. Heartland wants dispute resolved AND revised pricing aligned before end of April. Returns 7 emails for "Heartland."
OE 2: Search emails for Swift rate increase. Use search_emails(query: "Swift") or search_emails(query: "surcharge") or search_emails(query: "Greg Pallone") or search_emails(query: "hazmat"). Agent finds email from Greg Pallone (greg.pallone@swiftrelocations.com) detailing Swift's rate changes. Key details: 15% base rate increase on all new household-goods and corporate relocation bookings effective immediately (April 11), plus $500 per-shipment DOT documentation surcharge where crews manage added compliance paperwork. Rationale: extra administrative load from retroactive placarding certificates, chain-of-custody logs, and hazmat declarations that Swift's dispatch coordinators and drivers are now spending material time on. Note: pricing applies to new bookings only, not retroactively to existing in-progress moves. Returns 15 emails for "Swift."
OE 3: Search Slack for Heartland vendor discussions. Use conversations_search_messages(search_query: "Heartland") or conversations_search_messages(search_query: "rate increase"). Agent finds messages across channels discussing: Marcus's original dispute communication (March 25), Heartland's rate notice and scheduling threat, Hana's expanded Q1 review findings (additional discrepancies beyond original $4,800 — Kevin Tran $650 unauthorized storage, Priya Nair shuttle fee mismatch), references to vendor reconciliation tracker VRECON-2026-Q1-HM. Returns 20 messages for "Heartland."
OE 4: Search Slack for Swift vendor discussions. Use conversations_search_messages(search_query: "Swift") or conversations_search_messages(search_query: "surcharge") or conversations_search_messages(search_query: "hazmat"). Agent finds messages across channels discussing Swift's rate increase announcement, DOT compliance surcharge impacts on in-progress moves, driver paperwork pushback from Blessing in #operations, existing hazmat handling surcharges on individual moves. Returns 20 messages for "Swift."
OE 5: Search QuickBooks for vendor records. Use quickbooks_search_vendors(fetchAll: true). Note: criteria-based searches error in this environment — agent must fall back to fetchAll. Agent finds 7 vendors total, including vendor_heartland_movers (Heartland Movers, balance $12,800.00) and vendor_swift (Swift Relocations, balance $11,010.00).
OE 6: Search QuickBooks for outstanding bills. Use quickbooks_search_bills(fetchAll: true). Note: criteria-based searches error — agent must fall back to fetchAll. Agent finds 17 bills total. Key Heartland bills: bill_heartland_q1_2026_hold (HM-2026-Q1-0042, $12,800, FULL PAYMENT HOLD, DISPUTED, past due April 25); HM-2026-0418 (Abdi move, $2,150, due May 2). Key Swift bills: SR-2026-0419 (Kevin Tran/Vectral, $2,885, due May 3); SR-2026-0421 (Priya Venkatesh/GreenStack, $4,450, due May 4); SR-2026-0422 (Ines Kovac/PivotPoint, $3,675, due May 4). Other notable: ACCRUAL-2026-0415-MOSAIC ($90,000 contingent liability, Mosaic prototype damage, $40K direct MoveOps exposure); ACCRUAL-DOT-2026-001 ($50,000 DOT penalty accrual).
OE 7: Search Slack for margin analysis work. Prompt says "someone on my team was building the margin exposure analysis" — agent does NOT know this is Alejandro. Use conversations_search_messages(search_query: "margin") or conversations_search_messages(search_query: "margin impact") or search_emails(query: "margin model"). Agent discovers Alejandro Fuentes's findings: structural data reconciliation problem (Airtable relocation records, QuickBooks vendor bills, CRM pipeline data don't reconcile cleanly), can only produce directional estimates not auditable per-client numbers, estimated 6-8 percentage point margin compression on vendor-dependent moves. His Linear ticket (#214) is building the consolidated dataset. Agent must resolve: "someone on my team" → Alejandro Fuentes (needed for CC later). Returns 16 messages for "Alejandro."
OE 8: Search Slack/Email for backup vendor research. Prompt says "I vaguely remember someone was looking into alternatives" — agent does NOT know this is Chloe or Lakeline. Use conversations_search_messages(search_query: "backup vendor") or conversations_search_messages(search_query: "alternative vendor") or conversations_search_messages(search_query: "Lakeline") or search_emails(query: "Lakeline") or search_emails(query: "Sandra Walsh"). Agent discovers Chloe Vance's Lakeline Logistics findings via Sandra Walsh (Director of Operations): pricing ~10% below Heartland's current rates; coverage limited to Midwest and Southeast corridors (IL, MI, OH, IN, WI, MN, MO, GA, NC, SC, TN, FL, parts of TX; cities include Detroit, Chicago, Atlanta, Charlotte, Nashville, Orlando, Raleigh); no West Coast coverage, no international, cannot absorb large volume shifts quickly. Assessment: viable for targeted Midwest/Southeast overflow, not a national replacement. Agent must resolve: "someone was looking into alternatives" → Chloe Vance (needed for CC later). Returns 4 Slack messages for "Lakeline," 3 emails.
OE 9: Search Slack for client exposure mapping. Prompt says "someone asked the account managers to flag which clients would get hit hardest" — agent does NOT know this is David Chen. Use conversations_search_messages(search_query: "client exposure") or conversations_search_messages(search_query: "which clients") or conversations_search_messages(search_query: "account manager"). Agent discovers David Chen's request to AMs in #customer-engagement (C002) to map which clients would be hit hardest. Mina Hashimoto responded: Vectral = RED (trust-fragile, already negotiated 10% loyalty discount, cannot absorb pass-through), BrightLoop = RED (volume-fragile, May rollout, Swift already hit 2 moves with DOT surcharge). Emeka Diallo responded: Mosaic = YELLOW trending RED (schedule-pressure, $90K damage claim open, specialized handling). Catalina Dubois DID NOT respond — causing missing Midwest account coverage (NorthWind, Prairie Data, other Heartland-dependent accounts). Additional gaps: no assessments for Canopy Health, GreenStack, PivotPoint. Agent must resolve: "someone asked the AMs" → David Chen AND "whoever runs the client relationships" → David Chen (CE Lead).
OE 10: Connect Mosaic damage claim to Heartland financial exposure. Prompt explicitly asks "the Mosaic equipment damage claim sitting with Heartland... how that factors into our overall financial exposure with them." Agent discovers ACCRUAL-2026-0415-MOSAIC ($90,000) through QuickBooks bills search (OE 6). May also find context via conversations_search_messages(search_query: "Mosaic damage") or search_emails(query: "Mosaic prototype"). $90K contingent liability — $50K covered by insurance, $40K direct MoveOps exposure. Agent must synthesize: MoveOps owes Heartland for Mosaic damage WHILE disputing the $12,800 Q1 invoice.
OE 11: Search Slack/Email for Heartland dispute expanded scope. Prompt says "I filed the original dispute on three line items, but I've heard the problems may go deeper." Agent discovers full dispute picture through broader Heartland searches. Original dispute: $4,800 overbilling on 3 of 8 Q1 moves (Lily Marchetti $1,600 cancelled, Danielle Osei $1,600 cancelled, Priya Venkatesh $1,600 completed by Swift). Hana Kim's expanded review (April 14) found additional discrepancies: Kevin Tran $650 unauthorized storage charge, Priya Nair shuttle fee mismatch. Total disputed: ~$5,450+ out of $12,800. Heartland has NOT responded with corrected invoice.
OE 12: Search Linear for Heartland dispute tracking. Prompt says "make sure whatever we're using to track the Heartland dispute reflects where things actually stand" — agent does NOT know tracking is in Linear. Use linear_list_issues(query: "Heartland") or linear_list_issues(query: "reconciliation") or linear_list_issues(query: "Q1 billing"). Agent finds issue_heartland_q1_recon (ticket #342) — the reconciliation ticket for the Heartland Q1 billing dispute, with current state, assignee, priority, description and comments. Failure mode: agent may not realize "whatever we're using to track" implies a ticketing system and may skip Linear entirely.
OE 13: Look up contact details for write actions. Use contacts_search_contacts(query: "Elena"), contacts_search_contacts(query: "Chloe"), contacts_search_contacts(query: "Alejandro"), contacts_search_contacts(query: "David"). Agent obtains: elena.rostova@moveops.com (1 result), chloe.vance@moveops.com (1 result), alejandro.fuentes@moveops.com (1 result), david.chen@moveops.com (3 results for "David" — agent must identify the correct David Chen). Note: agent must FIRST discover Chloe = "ops side" and Alejandro = "margin analysis" before knowing to look up their contacts for CC.
OE 14: Look up Slack channels. Use channels_list. Agent finds C005 = #finance (9 channels total). Agent must infer that "the rest of the finance team" means #finance channel, not a DM.
OE 15: Update Heartland reconciliation ticket in Linear. Prompt says "make sure whatever we're using to track the Heartland dispute reflects where things actually stand now that rates and billing are tangled up." Use linear_update_issue(id: "issue_heartland_q1_recon", ...) or linear_create_comment(issueId: "issue_heartland_q1_recon", body: ...). Agent updates the ticket incorporating: rate increase now tied to Q1 dispute (Jake Loomis's formal notice), Hana's expanded findings (~$5,450+ disputed out of $12,800), current QuickBooks payment hold status, Mosaic damage complication, connection to vendor strategy memo deadline. Failure mode: agent may not realize "whatever we're using to track" means Linear and may skip this action.
OE 16: Send Elena the financial breakdown email with discovered CCs. Prompt says "Elena needs the complete financial breakdown, and loop in whoever's been handling the ops side of the vendor situation and whoever built the margin analysis." Use send_email(to: "elena.rostova@moveops.com", cc: ["chloe.vance@moveops.com", "alejandro.fuentes@moveops.com"], subject: relating to vendor rate increases and financial exposure, body: ...). CC resolution: "whoever's been handling the ops side" → Chloe Vance (discovered through Lakeline/vendor threads); "whoever built the margin analysis" → Alejandro Fuentes (discovered through margin Slack threads). Email covers: what each vendor is demanding (Heartland 20% effective July 1, Swift 15% + $500 DOT surcharge effective immediately), current vendor payment positions from QuickBooks (Heartland $12,800 on hold/disputed, Swift $11,010 clean), Heartland dispute expanded scope (~$5,450+ disputed), Mosaic damage claim ($90K accrual and its connection to Heartland exposure), margin impact estimates (6-8 points compression), per-client risk assessment (Vectral RED, BrightLoop RED, Mosaic YELLOW→RED), backup vendor option (Lakeline — Midwest/SE only, ~10% below Heartland), flagged data gaps (Alejandro's model directional only, Catalina's non-response on Midwest accounts). Failure modes: agent may not CC Chloe (doesn't connect "ops side" to her) or may not CC Alejandro (doesn't connect "margin analysis" to him).
OE 17: Inform client-facing team about per-client exposure. Prompt says "whoever runs the client relationships needs the per-client exposure picture before an account manager gets blindsided on a pricing question." Agent must resolve "whoever runs client relationships" → David Chen (CE Lead). Use send_email(to: "david.chen@moveops.com", subject: relating to client exposure from vendor rate increases, body: ...) or alternatively conversations_add_message to #customer-engagement (C002) or Slack DM to David. Email covers: what changed (both vendors' specific increases), client-by-client exposure with risk levels from AM responses, data gaps from Catalina's missing Midwest account coverage, guidance on client communication approach if pricing questions arise. Failure modes: agent doesn't figure out David Chen is the right recipient, or emails wrong person, or posts to wrong channel, or reports data in final response without separate communication.
OE 18: Post summary for finance team. Prompt says "the rest of the finance team needs to see the key numbers — someone over there has been doing their own reconciliation work on Heartland and needs to factor in the rate picture." Agent must resolve "rest of the finance team" → #finance channel (C005) and "someone doing reconciliation" → Hana Kim. Use conversations_add_message(channel_id: "C005", payload: ...). Summary covers: both vendors' rate increase terms, current vendor balances from QuickBooks, Heartland dispute expanded scope and status, margin compression estimates, backup vendor option (Lakeline), note about factoring in the rate picture for reconciliation work. Failure modes: agent may DM Hana instead of posting to #finance channel, may post to wrong channel (#general, #operations), or may skip this action entirely.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.
universe_idmcp-advanced-3-20-moveops-metadata

OE 1: Search emails for Heartland rate increase. Use search_emails(query: "Heartland") or search_emails(query: "rate increase") or search_emails(query: "Jake Loomis"). Agent finds email from Jake Loomis (jake.loomis@heartlandmovers.com), subject relating to "Formal Notice: Q3 2026 Rate Adjustment and Account Review" (or similar). Agent obtains: elena.rostova@moveops.com (1 result), chloe.vance@moveops.com (1 result), alejandro.fuentes@moveops.com (1 result), david.chen@moveops.com (3 results for "David" — agent must identify the correct David Chen). Note: agent must FIRST discover Chloe = "ops side" and Alejandro = "margin analysis" before knowing to look up their contacts for CC.
OE 14: Look up Slack channels. Use channels_list. Agent finds C005 = #finance (9 channels total). Agent must infer that "the rest of the finance team" means #finance channel, not a DM.
OE 15: Update Heartland reconciliation ticket in Linear. Prompt says "make sure whatever we're using to track the Heartland dispute reflects where things actually stand now that rates and billing are tangled up." Use linear_update_issue(id: "issue_heartland_q1_recon", ...) or linear_create_comment(issueId: "issue_heartland_q1_recon", body: ...). Agent updates the ticket incorporating: rate increase now tied to Q1 dispute (Jake Loomis's formal notice), Hana's expanded findings (~$5,450+ disputed out of $12,800), current QuickBooks payment hold status, Mosaic damage complication, connection to vendor strategy memo deadline. Failure mode: agent may not realize "whatever we're using to track" means Linear and may skip this action.
OE 16: Send Elena the financial breakdown email with discovered CCs. Prompt says "Elena needs the complete financial breakdown, and loop in whoever's been handling the ops side of the vendor situation and whoever built the margin analysis." Use send_email(to: "elena.rostova@moveops.com", cc: ["chloe.vance@moveops.com", "alejandro.fuentes@moveops.com"], subject: relating to vendor rate increases and financial exposure, body: ...). CC resolution: "whoever's been handling the ops side" → Chloe Vance (discovered through Lakeline/vendor threads); "whoever built the margin analysis" → Alejandro Fuentes (discovered through margin Slack threads). Email covers: what each vendor is demanding (Heartland 20% effective July 1, Swift 15% + $500 DOT surcharge effective immediately), current vendor payment positions from QuickBooks (Heartland $12,800 on hold/disputed, Swift $11,010 clean), Heartland dispute expanded scope (~$5,450+ disputed), Mosaic damage claim ($90K accrual and its connection to Heartland exposure), margin impact estimates (6-8 points compression), per-client risk assessment (Vectral RED, BrightLoop RED, Mosaic YELLOW→RED), backup vendor option (Lakeline — Midwest/SE only, ~10% below Heartland), flagged data gaps (Alejandro's model directional only, Catalina's non-response on Midwest accounts). Failure modes: agent may not CC Chloe (doesn't connect "ops side" to her) or may not CC Alejandro (doesn't connect "margin analysis" to him).
OE 17: Inform client-facing team about per-client exposure. Prompt says "whoever runs the client relationships needs the per-client exposure picture before an account manager gets blindsided on a pricing question." Agent must resolve "whoever runs client relationships" → David Chen (CE Lead). Use send_email(to: "david.chen@moveops.com", subject: relating to client exposure from vendor rate increases, body: ...) or alternatively conversations_add_message to #customer-engagement (C002) or Slack DM to David. Email covers: what changed (both vendors' specific increases), client-by-client exposure with risk levels from AM responses, data gaps from Catalina's missing Midwest account coverage, guidance on client communication approach if pricing questions arise. Failure modes: agent doesn't figure out David Chen is the right recipient, or emails wrong person, or posts to wrong channel, or reports data in final response without separate communication.
OE 18: Post summary for finance team. Prompt says "the rest of the finance team needs to see the key numbers — someone over there has been doing their own reconciliation work on Heartland and needs to factor in the rate picture." Agent must resolve "rest of the finance team" → #finance channel (C005) and "someone doing reconciliation" → Hana Kim. Use conversations_add_message(channel_id: "C005", payload: ...). Summary covers: both vendors' rate increase terms, current vendor balances from QuickBooks, Heartland dispute expanded scope and status, margin compression estimates, backup vendor option (Lakeline), note about factoring in the rate picture for reconciliation work. Failure modes: agent may DM Hana instead of posting to #finance channel, may post to wrong channel (#general, #operations), or may skip this action entirely.

2 The Linear update content (in the linear_update_issue description/comment or linear_create_comment body parameter) on the Heartland reconciliation ticket mentions the rate increase being connected or tied to the dispute resolution (or similar phrasing linking the 20% rate increase to the open billing dispute).

✗Run #2Although the Linear update content explicitly states 'RATE INCREASE TIED TO DISPUTE' and links Heartland's 20% increase to the unresolved Q1 billing dispute, the update was applied to ticket #118, not the required reconciliation ticket #342 (issue_heartland_q1_recon). Since the update targets the wrong ticket, this criterion is not satisfied.
✗Run #3Although the content the agent wrote in its Linear update does mention the rate increase being tied to the dispute ('Heartland issued a formal 20% rate increase notice for Q3 (July 1), and Jake Loomis explicitly conditioned preferred scheduling on resolving this dispute'), this update was applied to ticket #118, not the required #342 (issue_heartland_q1_recon). Since the update targeted the wrong ticket, this criterion is not satisfied.

10 The email body (in the send_email body parameter) to [elena.rostova@moveops.com](mailto:elena.rostova@moveops.com) mentions a margin compression or margin impact estimate (such as 6-8 percentage points, or a specific range/number indicating how much margins would shrink under the new rates).
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The email body provides specific margin compression figures: 'margin compresses from ~33% to ~23%' (10pp compression below the 25% floor) and 'margin drops from ~36% to ~15%', satisfying the requirement for a specific numeric estimate of margin reduction.
✓Run #2The email body to Elena states 'A 20% Heartland increase drops relocation-service margin from ~30% to roughly 16-17% on Heartland-fulfilled moves (BELOW our 25% floor)' and 'A 15% Swift increase + $500 surcharge drops margin to roughly 18-22% depending on move distance', providing specific ranges showing margin compression.
✓Run #3The email body to Elena states: 'A 20% Heartland increase alone compresses relocation margin to roughly 17-22% (below our 25% floor)' and 'Swift's combined increase hits harder on specialized moves: margin could drop to 10-15%'. This provides a numeric estimate of margin compression.
✓Run #4The email body to elena.rostova@moveops.com states margin compresses '~6-7 percentage points on Heartland-routed moves' and describes combined blended margins dropping to '22-25% range' under new vendor rates, satisfying the requirement for a margin compression estimate.
✓Run #5The email body to Elena includes specific margin compression estimates: 'Heartland's increase = ~$2,560/quarter compression' and 'Swift's new rates... could compress margins by $5,000-8,000/quarter depending on lane mix', satisfying the 'specific range/number indicating how much margins would shrink' criterion.
✓Run #6The email body to Elena mentions 'Estimated margin compression to ~23-24% on Heartland moves — BELOW the 25% floor policy' and similar margin compression for Swift moves, providing a specific margin impact estimate.

27 The model must use a QuickBooks tool (within the quickbooks\_\* namespace, such as quickbooks_search_vendors or quickbooks_search_bills) to look up vendor balances or billing records.
tool selection
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
